Module 2: The Business
Launch Formula, Part 2
!
Executive Summary
!
One of the fundamental things you should understand is that you will end
up with a bunch of different lists. This video is all about your list and how it
works within your business
There are two main types of lists:
• 	A prospect list
• 	A buyer (owner) list
When John Gallagher started he had just a newsletter list, which was a
prospect list. Once they bought his board game they moved to the buyers
(Owners) list.
Next let’s assume he was going to do a launch for his herbal kit. He would
then send an email to inform his list about the herbal kit product opportunity
and ask them to opt in - this would be sent to both the prospects and the
buyers of the board game.
If they purchase the herbal kit they would move to a new buyers list - so
you’ll have multiple buyers lists. Now he wants to sell his membership site -
again the process is repeated. An opportunity to opt in to his launch list is
sent to the prospects and buyers of the first two products.
!

-- 1 of 2 --

A person on your buyers list is worth 15-20X a person on your prospect list.
Once you sell something to a customer once, it’s a lot easier the second
time. Some people do launches to their list once a month.
To prevent your prospects from being overwhelmed or fatigued by launch
emails, you’ll encourage them to opt in to the specific launch list. You
should offer some form of great prelaunch content when asking them to opt
in.
When John first started out he did a full out launch to his entire list. but in
general you want to create a new launch list with each new product. This is
especially true if it’s different from your first product, or different from the
main reason they signed up for your list.
Every business is different and how you organize your list will be different.
A lead (new person on your list) is most responsive when they first opt in to
your list - over time they become less responsive.
When you do a launch and ask people to opt in again it usually
reinvigorates the list.
!
